Title: Successful user registration and login
Preconditions:
- App is launched.
- No existing user account for test credentials.
Test Steps:
1. Launch the app.
2. Attempt to log in with the provided credentials.
3. System detects no registered account and redirects to registration.
4. Enter username, email, and password in the registration form.
5. Submit the form.
6. System validates inputs and confirms successful registration.
7. Attempt to log in again with the same credentials.
Expected Result:
- User is successfully registered and logged in.
- Homepage is displayed after login.

Title: Failed login with invalid credentials
Preconditions:
- App is launched.
- User account exists (`username: validuser`, `password: ValidPass123!`).
Test Data:
- Invalid username: `wronguser`
- Valid username but wrong password: `validuser` / `WrongPass123!`
Test Steps:
1. Launch the app.
2. Attempt to log in with `wronguser` and any password.
3. Attempt to log in with `validuser` and `WrongPass123!`.
Expected Result:
- Both attempts fail; system shows error message (e.g., "Invalid credentials").
- User is not redirected to the homepage.

Title: Registration with invalid email format
Preconditions:
- App is launched.
- No existing user account.
Test Data:
- Username: `testuser456`
- Invalid email: `invalid.email` (missing `@` or domain)
- Password: `ValidPass456!`
Test Steps:
1. Launch the app.
2. Attempt to register with the invalid email.
3. Submit the form.
Expected Result:
- System rejects registration due to invalid email format.
- Error message appears (e.g., "Please enter a valid email address").
- User remains in registration flow.

Title: Successful event creation with valid inputs
Preconditions:
- User is logged in.
- User is on a city view screen (e.g., "Rome").
Test Data:
- Event title: `Tech Conference 2023`
- Date: `2023-12-15`
- Type: `Conference`
- Creator: `testuser123` (auto-filled from user session)
- City: `Rome`
- Description: `Annual tech conference with keynote speakers.`
Test Steps:
1. Scroll to the bottom of the city view and press `+` to add an event.
2. Fill all fields with the provided data.
3. Submit the form.
Expected Result:
- Event is created successfully.
- System displays confirmation (e.g., "Event created!").
- Event appears in the city’s event list.

Title: Event creation with missing required field
Preconditions: - User is logged in and on a city view screen.
Test Data:
- Event title: (left blank)
- Date: `2023-12-15`
- Type: `Conference`
- City: `Rome`
- Description: `Annual tech conference.`
Test Steps:
1. Scroll to the bottom and press `+` to add an event.
2. Leave the **title** field blank and fill the rest.
3. Submit the form.
Expected Result:
- System rejects submission due to missing title.
- Error message appears (e.g., "Title is required").
- User is prompted to re-enter the title.

Title: Event creation with invalid date format
Preconditions: - User is logged in and on a city view screen.
Test Data:
- Event title: `Invalid Date Event`
- Date: `31-02-2023` (non-existent date)
- Type: `Workshop`
- City: `Milan`
- Description: `Test event with invalid date.`
Test Steps:
1. Scroll to the bottom and press `+` to add an event.
2. Enter the invalid date and fill other fields.
3. Submit the form.
Expected Result:
- System rejects submission due to invalid date.
- Error message appears (e.g., "Please enter a valid date").
- User is prompted to correct the date.

Title: Successful event modification
Preconditions:
- User is logged in and on a city view screen.
- An existing event is listed (e.g., `Old Event`).
Test Data:
- Original event title: `Old Event`
- Modified title: `Updated Event Name`
- Modified description: `This event has been updated.`
Test Steps:
1. Scroll to the event list and press the **pencil icon** to modify the event.
2. Update the title and description.
3. Submit the form.
Expected Result:
- Event is updated successfully.
- System displays confirmation (e.g., "Event updated!").
- Event list reflects the changes.

Title: Event modification with invalid city selection
Preconditions:
- User is logged in and on the `Rome` city view.
- An existing event is listed (created in `Milan`).
Test Data:
- Original city: `Milan`
- Attempted city change: `Paris` (user is not on `Paris` view).
Test Steps:
1. Scroll to the event list and press the **pencil icon** to modify the event.
2. Attempt to change the city to `Paris` (not currently selected).
3. Submit the form.
Expected Result:
- System rejects the change or restricts city selection to the current view (`Rome`).
- Error message appears (e.g., "City must match the current view").
- Event remains unchanged.

Title: Successful event deletion
Preconditions:
- User is logged in and on a city view screen.
- An existing event is listed (e.g., `Test Event`).
Test Steps:
1. Scroll to the event list and press `>` to delete the event.
2. Confirm deletion in the prompt.
Expected Result:
- Event is deleted successfully.
- System displays confirmation (e.g., "Event removed!").
- Event no longer appears in the city’s event list.

Title: Attempt to delete non-existent event
Preconditions:
- User is logged in and on a city view screen.
- No events are listed.
Test Steps:
1. Scroll to the bottom of the event list (empty).
2. Attempt to press `>` to delete an event.
Expected Result:
- System prevents deletion (e.g., no `>` button appears or shows "No events to delete").
- No error occurs; user remains on the city view.

Title: Navigation to city view without login
Preconditions:
- App is launched.
- User is not logged in.
Test Steps: 1. Attempt to navigate directly to a city view (e.g., via URL or search).
Expected Result:
- System redirects to login/registration screen.
- User cannot access city view without authentication.

Title: Search for non-existent city
Test Steps:
1. Perform a city search with `ZzzCity999`.
2. Submit the search.
Expected Result:
- System returns no results or displays "No cities found."
- User remains on the search screen with an option to retry.

Title: POI association with city (implicit validation)
Preconditions: - System is running (no UI interaction needed; logical validation).
Test Steps:
1. (Logical) Assume a POI is created with `city = "Rome"` and `provincia = "RM"`.
2. Verify the POI is stored with the correct city association.
Expected Result:
- POI is correctly linked to `Rome` in the database.
- No validation errors occur during creation.

Title: POI with missing city
Test Steps: 1. (Logical) Attempt to create a POI without specifying a city.
Expected Result:
- System rejects the POI creation due to missing city.
- Error logged (e.g., "City is required for POI").

Title: User email update
Preconditions: - User is logged in (`username: testuser123`, `email: old@example.com`).
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to user profile settings.
2. Update email to `newemail@example.com`.
3. Submit changes.
Expected Result:
- Email is updated successfully.
- System sends confirmation (e.g., "Email updated!").
- `getMail()` returns `newemail@example.com`.

Title: User password update with weak password
Test Data:
- Current password: `OldPass123!`
- New password: `weak` (does not meet complexity rules).
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to user profile settings.
2. Enter current password and new weak password.
3. Submit changes.
Expected Result:
- System rejects the update due to weak password.
- Error message appears (e.g., "Password must be at least 8 characters with special characters").
- Password remains unchanged.
